scope_statement: Bounded packet for evidence-backed review decisions after the R2.3 hold decision. This packet is not a broad cleanup and does not authorize dependency mutation.

status_values.review_ready: Exact evidence is attached, but human review and CLI mutation are still required.
status_values.unit_design_required: Do not apply an edge until unit scope is changed or retired.
status_values.rejected: Do not apply this machine suggestion unless new evidence explicitly overturns it.
status_values.hold_for_later_phase: Useful issue, but outside the bounded R2.4 packet.

candidate_edges.decision_basis: A15's formula and target exercise explicitly require calculating percentage changes before calculating Ev.

evidence.supports: Students first calculate percentage change in price and quantity, then calculate price elasticity.

evidence.anchor: unit A15 procedure step 2; unit A38 procedure
evidence.supports: A15 uses percentage-change calculation; A38 is the reusable percentage-change skill.

candidate_edges.next_action: After human review, apply through CLI as A15 needs A38 if still accepted.
candidate_edges.proof_required_to_close: CLI mutation log plus regenerated DAG/needs reports.

candidate_edges.decision_basis: Human review chose A15 as the generic elasticity route; target exercise §2.1.3 treats cross elasticity as the same formula structure as Ev with different variables.

evidence.anchor: knowledge/course_blueprint_v4.md §2.1.3, target exercise subquestion b and difficulty notes
evidence.supports: Cross elasticity uses the same formula structure as Ev and classifies substitutes/complements.
Human reviewer selected A: A16 and A17 should both route through A15.

candidate_edges.next_action: After human review, apply through CLI as A16 needs A15 if still accepted.
candidate_edges.proof_required_to_close: CLI mutation log plus regenerated DAG/needs reports.

candidate_edges.decision_basis: Human review chose A15 as the generic elasticity route; target exercise §2.1.3 treats income elasticity as the same formula structure as Ev with different variables.

evidence.anchor: knowledge/course_blueprint_v4.md §2.1.3, target exercise subquestion a and difficulty notes
evidence.supports: Income elasticity uses percentage changes and classifies normal/inferior/luxury goods.
Human reviewer selected A: A16 and A17 should both route through A15.

candidate_edges.next_action: After human review, apply through CLI as A17 needs A15 if still accepted.
candidate_edges.proof_required_to_close: CLI mutation log plus regenerated DAG/needs reports.

candidate_edges.decision_basis: D06 is interpretation of price elasticity; target and exam evidence pair D06 interpretation with A15 calculation.

evidence.supports: The target exercise calculates Ev and immediately interprets elastic/inelastic demand.

evidence.anchor: ha-1022-a-23-2-o, opgave 1, q2 required_skills A15 and q4 required_skills D06
evidence.supports: The same opgave first calculates elasticity and then asks for elasticity interpretation.

candidate_edges.next_action: After human review, apply through CLI as D06 needs A15 if still accepted.
candidate_edges.proof_required_to_close: CLI mutation log plus regenerated DAG/needs reports.

candidate_edges.decision_basis: D04 is not a simple dependency edge. Goods classification belongs inside the relevant elasticity units: A15/D06 for elastic/inelastic demand, A17 for income-elasticity goods, and A16/D27 for cross-elasticity substitutes/complements.

evidence.supports: The target exercise embeds goods classification inside income and cross elasticity work rather than as a standalone concept.

evidence.anchor: ha-1022-a-25-2-o, opgave 2, q8 D04 and q9 A15/A17
evidence.supports: The exam evidence couples classification and elasticity calculation in adjacent questions.

evidence.anchor: Question 5: D04 Elasticity And Goods Classification
evidence.supports: Human reviewer decided there is no need for a separate elasticity-and-goods-classification unit.

candidate_edges.next_action: Do not add D04 needs. Prepare a later CLI-supported unit deprecate/redistribute plan.
candidate_edges.proof_required_to_close: Human-reviewed unit-design decision plus CLI deprecation or redistribution log.

candidate_edges.prerequisite_name: Accijnsopbrengst uit grafiek / Consumentensurplus en accijns
candidate_edges.decision_basis: D01 and D03 are advanced accijns/surplus applications, not prerequisites for basic demand/supply graph-shift reasoning.

evidence.supports: Human reviewer rejected D10 -> D01/D03.
Shift-versus-movement reasoning is introduced before accijns/surplus applications.

candidate_edges.next_action: Do not apply. Later graph-foundation work may propose a different prerequisite chain.
candidate_edges.proof_required_to_close: No mutation; preserve rejection unless new exact evidence overturns it.

candidate_edges.decision_basis: D03 is too specialized to be a prerequisite for the general cost-increase supply-shift skill.

evidence.supports: Human reviewer rejected D13 -> D03.

candidate_edges.next_action: Do not apply. Later graph-foundation work may propose a different prerequisite chain.
candidate_edges.proof_required_to_close: No mutation; preserve rejection unless new exact evidence overturns it.

candidate_edges.unit_name: Verschuiving versus beweging langs de curve
candidate_edges.prerequisite_name: Accijnsopbrengst uit grafiek / Consumentensurplus en accijns
candidate_edges.decision_basis: D32 is foundational graph interpretation; D01/D03 are later tax/surplus applications.

evidence.supports: Human reviewer rejected D32 -> D01/D03.
D32 is practiced as the basic shift-versus-movement distinction.

candidate_edges.next_action: Do not apply. Later graph-foundation work may propose a different prerequisite chain.
candidate_edges.proof_required_to_close: No mutation; preserve rejection unless new exact evidence overturns it.

a_domain_zero_needs.decision_basis: These are foundational math/graph calculation skills. Human review chose underbouw_assumed for foundational A-domain empty-needs units instead of artificial internal prerequisites.

evidence.supports: Human reviewer selected underbouw_assumed.

a_domain_zero_needs.next_action: After CLI support exists, set explicit zero_needs_status and assumed_prior_knowledge through the approved workflow.

a_domain_zero_needs.decision_basis: These A-domain units are not safely covered by the foundational underbouw rule. A18 and A42 are flagged because prior_learning is new_this_year; A43 has suspicious suggested edges.

evidence.anchor: Findings on A18, A42, and A43
evidence.supports: Evidence review marked these as concerning or suspicious.

a_domain_zero_needs.next_action: Keep out of R2.4 mutation queue; review in later targeted A-domain cleanup.

unit_design_decisions.decision: Do not add a prerequisite edge for D04. Treat D04 as a unit-scope problem: retire, merge, or redistribute its content into the relevant elasticity units.

evidence.supports: Goods classification appears as part of income and cross elasticity tasks.
Human reviewer decided separate D04 is not needed.

unit_design_decisions.proof_required_to_close: Later CLI-supported unit-design change, with references regenerated.

labor_market_second_pass.summary: Labor-market and unemployment sequencing should be handled as a reviewed sequence, not as blind application of R2.1 machine edges.

review_ready_edges.decision_basis: Krappe/ruime arbeidsmarkt uses beroepsbevolking, arbeidsaanbod, arbeidsvraag, and unemployment categories.

evidence.supports: §4.1.2 defines labor-force categories; §4.1.3 uses krappe/ruime arbeidsmarkt.

review_ready_edges.decision_basis: Types of unemployment require the base distinction between beroepsbevolking, niet-beroepsbevolking, and werkloze beroepsbevolking.

evidence.supports: §4.2.1 classifies unemployment types after §4.1.2 defines unemployment-rate categories.

review_ready_edges.decision_basis: Minimum wage is a binding minimum price in the labor market; students need binding price-regulation logic and labor-market equilibrium transfer.

evidence.supports: Minimum wage is described as direct transfer from minimum price mechanics to the labor market.
Exam asks effect of raising minimum wage on unemployment.

review_ready_edges.decision_basis: CAO wage above equilibrium is explained as similar to a minimum wage.

evidence.supports: CAO wage-setting is explicitly compared to minimum wage and its employment effect.

review_ready_edges.decision_basis: Loonstarheid explains persistent unemployment after labor-market imbalance; krappe/ruime market language is prerequisite vocabulary.

evidence.anchor: knowledge/course_blueprint_v4.md §4.2.3, target exercise subquestions c and e
evidence.supports: Loonstarheid is used to explain persistent unemployment and Dutch wage adjustment.

review_ready_edges.decision_basis: Macro wage rigidity units use the labor-market concept of loonstarheid.

evidence.supports: The target exercise groups I04 and I10 with loonstarheid.

evidence.anchor: vw-1022-a-23-1-o q22 and vw-1022-a-25-2-o q24, required_skills I10
evidence.supports: VWO exam questions use wage/GA-curve rigidity reasoning.

review_ready_edges.decision_basis: H14's procedure explicitly reasons through the multiplier and leakage channel.

evidence.anchor: unit H14 procedure step 3 references I14
evidence.supports: The procedure itself says extra consumption works through the multiplier and references I14.
Exam evidence confirms multiplier/leakage reasoning as an assessed skill.

held_items.reason: L03 is plausible for labor demand vocabulary, but §4.1.3 evidence more directly supports L05/L10 before L09. Keep L03 as a later review candidate rather than mutate now.

H13 is a minimum-wage cost-channel and international-competitiveness unit. Evidence supports needing prior cost/minimum-wage reasoning, but the minimal prerequisite should be decided with R3.1/R3.2 CLI review rather than in R2.4.

labor_market_second_pass.next_action: Use these labor-market edges as review-ready candidates after R3.1 confirms CLI and validation coverage.

remaining_backlog_policy.next_sprint: R3.1 Reference CLI And Documentation Completion
remaining_backlog_policy.block_reason: R3.2 still needs human review of this packet and R3.1 CLI/documentation readiness before protected reference mutation.
